macro_segments.title: Avodah Zarah Without Belief: The Debate Between Abaye and Rava

Rava's Distinction: Like Haman But Not Exactly Like Haman

macro_segments.title: Yehareg V'Al Ya'avor: When Must One Give Up One's Life?

micro_segments.title: The Question: If Ahavah Umiyirah Is Not Avodah Zarah, Why Is It Yehareg V'Al Ya'avor?

macro_segments.title: Kohen Gadol and Avodah Zarah: Does Intent Matter?

micro_segments.title: The Braita: Kohen Mashiach's Offering for Avodah Zarah

The Case of Shiggas Ma'aseh: What Counts as Unintentional Error?
Three Scenarios Examined: Synagogue vs. Idol vs. Respect Without Belief
Why Only Ahavah Umiyirah Works: The Unique Case for Abaye

macro_segments.title: Multiple Acts of Avodah Zarah: One Offering or Many?

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Zakkai's Teaching: Slaughtering, Burning, Pouring, Bowing in One Forgetfulness Equals One Offering

macro_segments.title: Learning Out Chiluk Melachos from Me'Achas Mehena

micro_segments.title: The Pasuk Me'Achas Mehena: One, From One, Many, From Many
Achas and Me'Achas: The Case of Shimon's Name
Heina and Meiheina: Primary Categories and Derivative Forms
Achas She'Hi Heina: One That Is Many—Forgetting Shabbos, Remembering Melachos

macro_segments.title: Why Can't We Learn Chiluk Avodas Kochavim from Me'Achas Mehena?

micro_segments.title: The Problem: Me'Achas Mehena Is About Different Korbanot for Different People
By Avodah Zarah Everyone Brings the Same She-Goat
Conclusion: We Learn Chiluk Avodot from Hishtachavah, Not from Me'Achas Mehena

micro_segments.title: The Braita: Shabbos Is Stricter in Having Chiluk Melachos for Multiple Acts
Other Mitzvos: When You Do the Same Sin Multiple Times, Only One Offering
Reverse Stringency: Other Mitzvos Liable Even Without Intent; Shabbos Exempt if Mis'asek
